MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Julie E. Hayashida of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Geary Pacific Corporation
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Timothy Hoofer

RULING:
DEFENDANT GEARY PACIFIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Prior to the hearing the Court disclosed her relationship with defendant's counsel but found that it would not affect her decision in this case. No objection was made by parties.
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Geary Pacific Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also did not sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. As to defendant's arguments regarding substantial factor causation, defendant did not properly raise such issue in its separate statement. Even assuming defendant had properly made it an issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating either that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that his exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness or that it negated such element of plaintiff's claims. Defendant's request for judicial notice was granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Leticia A. Ralls of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10070 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
